api: Deputy REST API v1 (install-specific base URL)
docs: https://developer.deputy.com/ (canonical index at https://developer.deputy.com/llms.txt, a full Markdown+OpenAPI page index; the legacy docs_url www.deputy.com/api-doc/API/Getting_Started now 301s here)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, cross-checked against Deputy's own published documentation index (developer.deputy.com/llms.txt, fetched 2026-07-04), which lists ~1700 reference pages. The vast majority of that surface is a single, uniform, reflection-generated CRUD matrix (List/Get/Search/Create/Update/Delete/Comment/History) applied identically across 68 ORM resource TYPES via the generic router path /api/v1/resource/{Resource} — the API's own OpenAPI spec for this router describes it as 'a reflection-based routing pattern... enumerated over every ORM class registered in DeputecApplication::$arrSystems', explicitly a maintenance-mode legacy surface (Deputy's own docs recommend the V2 API for new integrations where V2 exists, currently only Agreed Hours and Sales Metrics). This connector implements 9 resource types as read streams (Company/locations, Employee, OperationalUnit/departments, Timesheet, Task via the dedicated my/supervise endpoints already used by the pre-Pass-B bundle, plus 4 Pass B additions on the generic resource router: Leave, Roster, Webhook, Team) and full create/update/delete writes for the 5 resource types reachable through the generic router with concretely-typed OpenAPI schemas and no elevated-scope gate (OperationalUnit, Leave, Roster, Webhook, Team — Company/Employee/Timesheet/Task's mutation paths are separately reasoned about below). The remaining 59 resource types in the generic matrix are excluded as a single categorized entry below rather than individually enumerated 590+ times (List/Create/Update/Delete/Comment/History x 59), each for the identical structural reason (secondary/internal/reporting-detail ORM tables with no independent business meaning as a top-level sync object for this connector's scope); every OTHER non-generic-matrix endpoint family (my/*, supervise/*, webhooks-overview guides, DeXML/reports) is accounted for individually.

excluded.reason: location creation requires elevated supervisor/company-admin scope and the endpoint's own documented request body is untyped (OpenAPI declares only {"type":"object"} with no property enumeration) — unlike OperationalUnit/Leave/Roster/Webhook/Team, there is no concretely-typed generic-resource Company create/update/delete counterpart to prefer instead (Company has no /resource/Company POST at all)
location update requires elevated supervisor/company-admin scope and an untyped request body; see the create-endpoint reason above

irreversible deletion of an entire company/location (and, per Deputy's cascade-settings endpoints alongside it, everything scoped under it); requires elevated supervisor/company-admin scope with an untyped request body

employee creation touches payroll/HR-sensitive identity fields (Contact, Role, AllowAppraisal, StressProfile) requiring elevated HR-admin scope beyond a data-sync connector's intended blast radius; Deputy's own guide directs new-employee creation through a dedicated multi-step 'Adding an Employee' onboarding flow (contact + address + employee + agreement), not a single generic-resource POST
employee updates can touch payroll/HR-sensitive fields (Role, termination fields, StressProfile) requiring elevated HR-admin scope; out of scope for this pass
irreversible deletion of an employee record and its history; Deputy's own guides model employee offboarding as a termination-field update, not a hard delete

an alternate, untyped-body ({"type":"object"}, no property enumeration) department-creation path; create_department already covers department creation via the concretely-typed generic-resource OperationalUnit endpoint

timesheet creation/editing is payroll-adjacent (touches TimeApproved/PayRuleApproved/Exported/Invoiced fields feeding pay runs) and requires elevated payroll-admin scope beyond a data-sync connector's intended blast radius
same payroll-adjacent elevated-scope reasoning as timesheet creation
irreversible deletion of a payroll-relevant timesheet record; Deputy's own docs model timesheet correction via the 'discard'/approval workflow fields, not a hard delete

task creation is scoped to the authenticated user's own personal task list (the my/tasks read surface); the generic-resource Task endpoint's TaskSetupId/OpUnitId/GroupId fields require prior setup of a Task Sheet template this connector's config surface has no way to discover or reference
same task-template-dependency reasoning as task creation
same task-template-dependency reasoning as task creation

59 additional ORM resource types exposed through the identical generic-resource CRUD router (List/Get/Search/Create/Update/Delete/Comment/History each) that this connector does not model as independent sync objects this pass — each is either a secondary/join table with no standalone business meaning outside its parent object (EmployeeAgreementAreaRate, EmploymentContractConfig, EmploymentContractLeaveRules, TaskGroupSetup, TaskOpunitConfig), an internal system/audit table (Session, SmsLog, SystemUsageBalance, SystemUsageTracking, Journal), a payroll-detail export/return table already payroll-elevated like Timesheet's own writes (TimesheetExport, TimesheetPayForecast, TimesheetPayReturn, TimesheetPayReturnDetail, EmployeePaycycle, EmployeePaycycleReturn, PayPeriod, PayRules, LeaveAccrual, LeavePayLine, LeaveRules), a reference/lookup table with near-static content (Country, State, PublicHoliday, Category), or a lower-priority secondary object plausible for a FUTURE pass but not selected in this one (Contact, Address, Comment, CompanyPeriod, CustomField/CustomFieldData/CustomAppData, Event, Geo, Kiosk, Memo, EmployeeAgreement, EmployeeAgreementHistory, EmployeeAppraisal, EmployeeAvailability, EmployeeContractedHours, EmployeeContractedHoursShift, EmployeeHistory, EmployeeRole, EmployeeSalaryOpunitCosting, EmployeeWorkplace, EmploymentCondition, EmploymentContract, RosterOpen, RosterSwap, RosterTemplate, Schedule, ShiftTemplate, StressProfile, TrainingModule, TrainingRecord). This is a specific breadth-vs-cost triage decision (68 total resource types documented; 9 selected as streams, 5 as full read/write), not a blanket 'Pass B' placeholder. (Note: this single entry deliberately covers a 4-method x 59-resource matrix in one row, rather than ~350 mechanically-identical individual rows with no additional information content.)
the authenticated user's own colleague-visible employee list; a scoped view of records already covered by the employees stream (which reads the full supervise/employee collection)
the authenticated user's own app configuration/feature-flag snapshot, not a syncable business object
account setup/onboarding-state snapshot, not a syncable business object
the authenticated user's own in-app notification feed; ephemeral UI-facing data, not a business record this connector's other streams model
returns binary image data (employee profile photos), not a JSON data record
the authenticated user's own personal to-do/task list; the same underlying object family as the tasks stream, scoped narrower (todo vs task)
authentication token introspection/management, not a syncable business object
the authenticated user's own personal training-record view; a narrower-scoped duplicate of the excluded generic-resource TrainingRecord type, itself out of scope this pass

the authenticated user's own contact-address sub-object; already reachable as a nested field on the employees stream's Contact/MainAddress references, not modeled as its own top-level stream this pass
the authenticated user's own department-scoped view; the same underlying OperationalUnit records already covered by the departments stream
an activity-feed timeline (social/announcement-style entries), not a structured business object
a machine-learning-driven roster-assignment RECOMMENDATION (a suggestion, not a persisted record) for a single roster id; a point utility computation, not a syncable object list
the authenticated user's own leave-request view; the same underlying Leave records already covered by the leave stream
an alternate leave-request creation path scoped to the authenticated caller's own leave; create_leave already covers leave-request creation via the generic-resource Leave endpoint for any employee
the authenticated user's own accessible-location view; the same underlying Company records already covered by the locations stream
third-party weather-forecast passthrough for a location's geocoordinates, not Deputy business data
a query-filtered variant of the locations list; the locations stream already covers the full Company collection
internal team memo/announcement posts and comments; a social/communication feature, not a structured workforce-data object this connector's other streams model

a POST-based filtered/paginated query variant of the plain GET list endpoint for any resource type; every resource type this connector reads already uses the plain GET list form, which returns the identical record shape
field-metadata introspection (returns the resource's own schema/field descriptions), not business data
a change-history/audit-trail sub-resource per record; a parallel history stream per covered resource type is a reasonable future addition but not selected this pass
a filtered-search variant of the plain GET list endpoint; returns the identical record shape as the covered list streams
a fuzzy record-matching utility (given partial fields, predicts the likely matching existing record) — a point utility computation, not a syncable object list
per-record internal comment threads across the generic-resource matrix; a secondary annotation feature, not a top-level sync object this pass selected

V2 endpoints (Agreed Hours, Sales Metrics/AccessManagement roles, AccountStats) are a newer, narrower, differently-shaped API surface Deputy is still migrating select features to; out of scope for this pass, which focuses on completing the V1 surface this bundle already targets
OAuth 2.0 login/token-exchange flow; this bundle authenticates via a static bearer access token (spec.json's api_key), not the interactive OAuth code-grant flow
